  • Method Details

    • getFootprint

      public int getFootprint()
      Returns the memory footprint, that is the memory segment id. The reason for returning the memory segment id is to have the pointer addressing this memory (and therefore used with ATTACHMAIN and FREEMAIN commands) with a value that is the memory segment id For example: - Task 1 - GETMAIN returns a shared memory segment which id is 5. The returned pointer will have therefore value 5 (that is 5+0, where 5 is the footprint and 0 is the offset of the first byte of the area). - the pointer value (5) is stored in CWA - Task 2 - the pointer value (5) is read from CWA - the pointer is passed to ATTACHMAIN command that attaches indeed the shared memory segment 5
      Overrides:
      getFootprint in class Memory
      Returns:
      the memory footprint
